ambiguities.text: I can quickly submit the initial attendance to the faculty for review
the administration and faculty-in-charge are immediately aware of schedule changes
ambiguities.explanation: No specific time bound — 'immediately' is subjective
ambiguities.suggested_rewrite: within [X] seconds of the triggering event

ambiguities.text: If attendance is submitted by the CR, the card shows "Pending Review" with counts (e.g., "Present: 45 / Absent: 5").
ambiguities.explanation: Example given but exhaustive list may be needed
ambiguities.suggested_rewrite: [confirm if this is exhaustive or provide complete list]

ambiguities.text: Faculty can override any student's status (e.g., change "Absent" to "Present").
ambiguities.explanation: Example given but exhaustive list may be needed
ambiguities.suggested_rewrite: [confirm if this is exhaustive or provide complete list]

ambiguities.text: I can quickly understand my teaching progress and engagement
For each individual student, shows a detailed, date-wise attendance record along with a summary count (e.g., "Attended 8 out of 12 classes").
ambiguities.explanation: Example given but exhaustive list may be needed
ambiguities.suggested_rewrite: [confirm if this is exhaustive or provide complete list]

ambiguities.text: view real-time engagement metrics for all classes
The dashboard displays real-time engagement percentage (e.g., "85% engaged").
ambiguities.explanation: Example given but exhaustive list may be needed
ambiguities.suggested_rewrite: [confirm if this is exhaustive or provide complete list]

gaps.description: US001 has 5 ACs but none contain testable conditions (If/When/Then)
gaps.recommendation: Add conditional logic (e.g., 'If X, then the system shall Y') to make ACs verifiable

gaps.description: US001 has no ACs covering error/failure scenarios
gaps.recommendation: Add ACs for edge cases: What happens on network failure? Invalid input? Timeout?
